title: Carbon and its Compounds
summary: Chapter 4 explores the versatile nature of carbon and the vast range of organic compounds it forms. Carbon's ability to form bonds with itself (catenation) and with other atoms (tetravalency) creates millions of compounds. Students study hydrocarbons (saturated and unsaturated), functional groups, homologous series, nomenclature, and important carbon compounds like ethanol and ethanoic acid. Chemical properties including combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reactions are covered. Soaps and detergents and their cleansing action are also studied.
learningPoints: Explain catenation and tetravalency of carbon
Distinguish saturated (alkanes) from unsaturated (alkenes, alkynes) hydrocarbons
Draw structural formulae of organic compounds
Define homologous series and its characteristics
Write combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reactions of carbon compounds
Explain esterification: alcohol + acid → ester + water
Describe properties and uses of ethanol and ethanoic acid
Explain cleansing action of soaps (micelle formation)

laws.name: Catenation
laws.statement: Carbon has unique ability to bond with other carbon atoms to form long chains, branched chains and rings. This property is called catenation.

laws.name: Tetravalency
laws.statement: Carbon has valency 4 — it can form 4 covalent bonds. It can form single bonds (C-C), double bonds (C=C), and triple bonds (C≡C).

formulas.label: Combustion of Alkane
formulas.description: Complete combustion of methane produces CO₂ and H₂O.

formulas.label: Esterification
formulas.description: Ethanoic acid + Ethanol → Ethyl ethanoate (ester) + Water. Conc. H₂SO₄ catalyst.

formulas.label: Saponification
formulas.description: Hydrolysis of fat/ester with NaOH gives soap (sodium salt of fatty acid).

formulas.label: Addition Reaction
formulas.description: Hydrogen adds across double bond of ethene to form ethane. Ni catalyst.

formulas.label: Dehydration of Ethanol
formulas.description: Ethanol heated at 443K with conc. H₂SO₄ forms ethene (alkene).

formulas.label: Homologous Series Formula
formulas.expression: Alkanes: CₙH₂ₙ₊₂ | Alkenes: CₙH₂ₙ | Alkynes: CₙH₂ₙ₋₂
formulas.description: General molecular formula for each homologous series.

diagrams.title: Micelle Formation in Soap
diagrams.description: Hydrophobic tails point inward toward oil droplet; hydrophilic ionic heads point outward toward water. Dirt (oil) is trapped inside micelle and washed away.

diagrams.title: Structural Formulae of n-butane and Isobutane
diagrams.description: n-butane: straight chain C₄H₁₀. Isobutane: branched C₄H₁₀ with CH₃ group on C2.

resources.label: Karnataka SSLC Science Textbook
resources.description: Official Karnataka SSLC Science textbook. Chapter 4 covers Carbon and its Compounds.

resources.label: NCERT Science Textbook Class 10
resources.description: NCERT Class 10 Science Chapter 4 Carbon and its Compounds.

resources.label: Carbon and its Compounds — Video Lessons
resources.description: Khan Academy videos on organic chemistry, hydrocarbons, and functional groups.

pyqs.question: A property of carbon that is responsible for giving rise to large molecules is: (A) tetravalency (B) catenation (C) inflammability (D) allotropism
pyqs.answer: (B) catenation — carbon's ability to bond with itself forms long chains and large molecules.

pyqs.question: Write the structural formula of an unsaturated carbon compound containing four carbon atoms and a ketone functional group.
pyqs.answer: CH₃-CO-CH₂-CH₃ (Butan-2-one). The ketone group (-CO-) is between C2 and the chain.

pyqs.question: Write the structures of n-butane and isobutane. What is esterification? How is ethene obtained from ethanol?
pyqs.answer: n-butane: CH₃-CH₂-CH₂-CH₃. Isobutane: CH₃-CH(CH₃)-CH₃. Esterification: Alcohol+carboxylic acid react to form ester (CH₃COOH + C₂H₅OH → CH₃COOC₂H₅ + H₂O). Ethene from ethanol: CH₃CH₂OH → CH₂=CH₂ + H₂O (443K, conc. H₂SO₄).

pyqs.question: Explain the cleansing action of soap on dirt.
pyqs.answer: Soap molecules have hydrophilic (ionic) end and hydrophobic (carbon chain) end. Ionic end interacts with water; carbon chain with oil. Soap forms micelles with dirt inside. Micelles are washed away by water.

pyqs.question: The functional group present in pentanal is: (A) -OH (B) -CHO (C) -COOH (D) -CO-
pyqs.answer: (B) -CHO. Pentanal = CH₃CH₂CH₂CH₂CHO. The -al suffix indicates aldehyde (-CHO) functional group.

pyqs.question: Carbon atoms do not form C⁴⁺ cation or C⁴⁻ anion. Why?
pyqs.answer: To form C⁴⁺: needs to lose 4 electrons — requires enormous energy. To form C⁴⁻: gaining 4 electrons is difficult as 6 protons cannot hold 10 electrons stably. So carbon forms covalent bonds instead.

mcqs.question: The property of carbon to bond with itself to form long chains is called:
mcqs.explanation: Catenation is the unique ability of carbon to form bonds with other carbon atoms to form chains, rings, and branched structures.

mcqs.question: The functional group present in pentanal is:
mcqs.explanation: Pentanal = C₅ chain with aldehyde (-CHO) at end. -al suffix = aldehyde.

mcqs.question: General formula of alkenes is:
mcqs.explanation: Alkenes CₙH₂ₙ have one double bond. Alkanes CₙH₂ₙ₊₂. Alkynes CₙH₂ₙ₋₂.

mcqs.question: Esterification is a reaction between:
mcqs.explanation: CH₃COOH + C₂H₅OH → CH₃COOC₂H₅ + H₂O. Ester has fruity smell. Used in perfumes and flavouring.

mcqs.question: Ethene is obtained from ethanol by:
options: B. Dehydration with conc. H₂SO₄ at 443K
mcqs.explanation: CH₃CH₂OH → CH₂=CH₂ + H₂O. Conc. H₂SO₄ acts as dehydrating agent at 443K.

mcqs.question: In soap micelle during cleansing, dirt is trapped:
mcqs.explanation: Hydrophobic tails point toward oil/dirt inside micelle. Ionic heads face outward toward water.

mcqs.question: Which substance does not undergo chemical change and is used in addition reaction of alkenes?
mcqs.explanation: Ni (or Pd) is used as catalyst in hydrogenation: CH₂=CH₂ + H₂ → CH₃-CH₃. Ni itself doesn't change.

mcqs.question: Saponification is:
options: A. Making ester from acid and alcohol
B. Hydrolysis of ester with NaOH to form soap
mcqs.explanation: Fat + NaOH → Soap + Glycerol. This is saponification — basis of soap making.

mcqs.question: How many single bonds and double bonds are in C₃H₇COOH?
mcqs.explanation: C₃H₇COOH (butanoic acid): 3 C-C + 3×3 + 1 C-C(COOH) + C-O-H = 12 single bonds, 1 C=O double bond.

mcqs.question: The simplest hydrocarbon is:
mcqs.explanation: Methane CH₄ is the simplest hydrocarbon. It has 1 carbon and 4 hydrogens.

mcqs.question: Detergents are better than soaps in hard water because:
options: A. Detergents form scum with Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺
B. Detergents do not form scum in hard water
mcqs.explanation: Soaps react with Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺ forming insoluble scum. Detergents (sulphonate group) don't form scum.

mcqs.question: Ethanoic acid is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: Ethanoic acid = acetic acid (CH₃COOH). Found in vinegar (5% solution). Melting point 17°C (glacial acetic acid).

mcqs.question: Substitution reaction occurs in:
mcqs.explanation: Saturated hydrocarbons (alkanes) undergo substitution reactions. CH₄ + Cl₂ → CH₃Cl + HCl (in sunlight).

mcqs.question: Addition reaction occurs in:
mcqs.explanation: Unsaturated compounds (alkenes, alkynes) with double/triple bonds undergo addition reactions.

mcqs.question: Glacial acetic acid is:
options: D. Frozen acetic acid only in winter
mcqs.explanation: Pure anhydrous acetic acid is called glacial acetic acid (melts at 17°C — ice-like crystals at room temp).

exercises.question: Draw structural formulae of: (i) Propanone (ii) Butanal (iii) Ethanoic acid
exercises.answer: (i) Propanone: CH₃-CO-CH₃ (ii) Butanal: CH₃-CH₂-CH₂-CHO (iii) Ethanoic acid: CH₃-COOH

exercises.question: Explain the cleansing action of soap with diagram.
exercises.answer: Soap has hydrophilic head (ionic, -COO⁻Na⁺) and hydrophobic tail (long carbon chain). In water, hydrophobic tails cluster around oil droplet forming a micelle. Ionic heads face outward. Micelles are stable in water and washed away with dirt. Diagram shows micelle cross-section.

exercises.question: Why are saturated hydrocarbons less reactive than unsaturated?
exercises.answer: Saturated hydrocarbons (alkanes) have only strong C-C and C-H single bonds. No multiple bonds available for addition. They undergo only substitution reactions with halogens in presence of UV light. Unsaturated (alkenes/alkynes) have reactive double/triple bonds that readily undergo addition reactions.

exercises.question: Write reactions of ethanol with: (i) Sodium (ii) Oxidation (iii) Conc. H₂SO₄ at 443K
exercises.answer: (i) 2C₂H₅OH + 2Na → 2C₂H₅ONa + H₂↑ (ii) C₂H₅OH → CH₃COOH (oxidation with alkaline KMnO₄) (iii) C₂H₅OH → CH₂=CH₂ + H₂O (dehydration)

exercises.question: What is a homologous series? Write the first four members of the alcohol series.
exercises.answer: Homologous series: group of compounds with same functional group, same general formula, differ by CH₂ unit. Properties vary gradually. Alcohol series: CH₃OH (methanol), C₂H₅OH (ethanol), C₃H₇OH (propanol), C₄H₉OH (butanol).

exercises.question: Why is ethanol considered harmful for health?
exercises.answer: Ethanol depresses the central nervous system. Drinking leads to: slowed reflexes, impaired judgment, liver damage (cirrhosis), addiction. Methanol (often mixed with ethanol illegally) causes blindness and death.

exercises.question: Write the dot structure of: (i) Nitrogen molecule (ii) Propyne
exercises.answer: (i) N≡N: N has one lone pair each side, triple bond between them. Dot structure shows :N:::N: (ii) Propyne: H-C≡C-C-H₃. First C has 1H and triple bond; second C has triple bond; third C has 3H.

exercises.question: Distinguish between soaps and detergents.
exercises.answer: Soaps: sodium/potassium salts of fatty acids, from natural fats, form scum in hard water, biodegradable. Detergents: sodium salts of sulphonic acids, from petroleum, effective in hard water, some non-biodegradable causing water pollution.
